title: Preparation of 0.5L of phosphate buffer (pH=6.0)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2kj6m","text":"Original source of the protocol: WormBook Methods http://www.wormbook.org/chapters/www_strainmaintain/strainmaintain.html","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":50,"length":71}],"data":{}},{"key":"31euv","text":"This protocol is for making pH=6.0 phosphate buffer, which is used in the preparation of worm media, like NGM (Nematode Growth Medium). materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"dtsi7","text":"Monopotassium phosphate:   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":25,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"fvrpi","text":"Dipotassium phosphate:   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":23,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"8stvl","text":"Filter: Rapid-Flow Nalgene 0.2µm aPES membrane, ref: 564-0020","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"75fd0","text":"Vaccum pump: Fisher Brand, ref: FB70155","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":956574,"guid":"4372CCE8341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Potassium phosphate monobasic","mol_weight":0,"sku":"795488-500G","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":957178,"guid":"437433D6341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Potassium phosphate dibasic","mol_weight":0,"sku":"P3786-500G","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Add 54.2 g, ±0.5 of monopotassium phosphate 1 (KH 2 PO 4 ) to a clean 0.5L bottle. 1 Also referred to as potassium phosphate monobasic
Add 17.8 g, ±0.2 of dipotassium phosphate 2 (K 2 HPO 4 ) to the bottle. 2 Also referred to as potassium phosphate dibasic
Measure 500 mL of milliQ water in a measuring cylinder, and add it to the bottle.
Shake the bottle vigorously until all crystals are dissolved. This can take a few minutes.
If this is the first time you prepare this solution, you can ensure that the pH is around 6 using pH paper or a pH-meter. We usually have a pH between 5.8 and 6.
Store on shelf. We usually use it without problem for at least a year after preparation.
creator: APE Lab
